content.problemStatement:
Given 222 parameters XXX and YYY and an array BBB of length MMM, define f(B,X)=(((Xmod  B1)mod  B2)…mod  BM)f(B, X) = (((X \mod B_1) \mod B_2) \ldots \mod B_M)f(B,X)=(((XmodB1​)modB2​)…modBM​).

The array BBB is called good if it is possible to rearrange BBB in some way such that f(B,X)=Yf(B, X) = Yf(B,X)=Y.

You are given an array AAA of length NNN, and 222 parameters XXX and YYY.

Find all KKK such that the prefix of array AAA with length KKK is good, i.e. [A1,A2,…,AK][A_1, A_2, \ldots, A_K][A1​,A2​,…,AK​] is good. Print these values of KKK in increasing order.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains NNN, XXX and YYY.
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.




Output Format
For each test case, output the following:



on the first line, output the number of prefixes that are good, say LLL.
on the second line, output LLL integers, K1K_1K1​, K2,…,KLK_2, \ldots, K_LK2​,…,KL​ - where each KiK_iKi​ is distinct and is the length of a good prefix. Further, the elements must be printed in ascending order, i.e. Ki<Ki+1K_i < K_{i + 1}Ki​<Ki+1​.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 400400400.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : K=4K = 4K=4 is good because we can rearrange B=[A1,A2,A3,A4]=[3,12,5,4]B = [A_1, A_2, A_3, A_4] = [3, 12, 5, 4]B=[A1​,A2​,A3​,A4​]=[3,12,5,4] to [12,4,5,3][12, 4, 5, 3][12,4,5,3]. We can verify ((((10mod  12)mod  4)mod  5)mod  3)=2((((10 \mod 12) \mod 4) \mod 5) \mod 3) = 2((((10mod12)mod4)mod5)mod3)=2.
Test Case 2 : There are no good prefixes.
